Satsang with Mooji
So we should remember our good fortune.

So here it is said that when one engages in these nine devotional activities, so our, as devotees, our endeavour should be, every moment should be engaged in these nine activities, one of these nine activities.
Either we are hearing, or we are chanting, or we are remembering, or we are serving, or we are praying.
And when one does that, he will be free from the influence of the modes of material nature.
Modes of material nature is like an imprisonment.
Yesterday we talked about the bodily concept of life is an imprisonment.
The modes of material nature force us to get into, come down from the spiritual platform to the material platform.
When one starts thinking in terms of,
I and mine, body and the associated bodily relatives.
One time, Shankaracharya, he was looking for a key outside his house, in the street, underneath a street light.
He had lost the key.
And he was desperately trying to find out where.
And one of his disciples came back to the ashram and saw Shankaracharya himself, kneeling and looking for the key, underneath the street light.
So naturally, he also said, let me also, he came to know that he has lost the key.
So he also started searching for the key.
And it went on for some time, 10 minutes, 15 minutes.
And then the disciple, you know, asked Shankaracharya, do you remember?
Do you remember where you lost the key?
You know, it is natural when we, this question comes in mind.
Do you remember vaguely, where do you remember?
So Shankaracharya, yeah, yeah, yeah, I remember.
It was somewhere inside, next to the bed.
So the disciple was a little amused.
He says, you lost the key inside, near the bed.
And why are you searching here?
So he said,
Inside, because the oil got over in the lamp, it became very dark.
So I thought, let me search the key outside, where there is light.
So the disciple, you know, was really, how can, I mean, he didn't say, but it was apparent from his looks.
How can he be so foolish?
Lost the key inside and is looking for the key outside, in the light.
So Shankaracharya just smiled at,
when the disciple was, you know, wonderstruck with this answer.
So Shankaracharya smiled.
And he said, isn't this the case with all of us?
This is a very important lesson.
Actually, living entity
is intrinsically happy.
Sat, chit, and ananda.
He doesn't need anything else.
He is amsa of the Lord, in association with the Lord.
He is completely happy.
Joyful.
Experiencing blissful relationship, reciprocal relationship with the Lord.
That is the quality of the jiva.
Actually, all of us, we are not happy with the jiva.
We are looking for happiness in the external world through sense gratification and so many other elaborate arrangements for that happiness.
Hoping against hope that I will be happy by this arrangement, by that arrangement, by this achievement, by this accomplishment.
And that's why the goalposts keep changing.
That's why nobody is happy.
Krishna in the Gita says, third chapter,
when one is self-realized,
when he is illumined from within,
when he is self-satisfied, he is happy within,
he has no duty to perform.
And how do we find that happiness?
How do we be situated in that intrinsic state of sat, chit, and ananda?
When we are free from the modes of material nature and when we are serving the Supreme Lord.
Thus, by engaging in the service of the, not by engaging in the service of modes of material nature,
by developing Krishna consciousness, which is nothing but the jiva realizing that I am amsha of the Lord,
I am eternally servant of the Lord,
jiveyera swaroopahoye nityera krishnadasa,
knowledge in renunciation.
Naturally, when we are free from the modes of material nature, we are serving the Supreme Lord.
Naturally, when one develops this knowledge that I am amsha of the Lord,
my duty is to serve the Lord,
naturally, jnana will result in vairagya.
Automatically, we will not serve false masters.
When we realize my true master is Krishna,
I am amsha of him, my duty is to serve him,
automatically, we will not want to serve false masters.
In which the mind is always fixed in devotional service,
so I have brought in the Supreme Lord in my life.
Compared to the Supreme person I have regarded,
one achieves my association in this very life.
So we all are in this business, this business of reviving our,
achieving our lost happiness.
And how the process is very simple,
we have to revive our Krishna consciousness.
So Prabhupāda says a devotee, if he has an opportunity to serve, be in association of devotees, and take little prasādam, he should be happy.
If we are not happy, that means we are under the influence of the modes.
No doubt, it's a fact that we cannot suddenly get out of the modes of material nature.
We are conditioned for many, many lifetimes. It will take. We are in the process of purification.
But, otherwise, for our happiness, we don't need elaborate arrangements, we don't need some big things to happen, we don't need big accomplishments, we don't need name, fame, adoration, position.
Intensically, that happiness is there.
And if we are not experiencing, we should understand, sometimes it becomes obvious that I have anarthas, I am lusty.
Give factor in that. Sometimes we blame somebody else outside.
I am not happy, not because of him, not because of this, not because of that. I am happy because I have anarthas.
I am still in bodily concept. Still, I am overpowered by modes of material nature.
Then, in one sense, it is empowering because otherwise, we blame others and empower others.
They are controlling me. Because of them, I am unhappy. Because of this, I am not experiencing happiness.
The answer is, it's within you.
Shila Prabhupada once, this was in 1977, he was in Vrindavan.
And as we know, his health was not quite good.
And he was taking some kind of Ayurvedic treatment.
And his health was generally deteriorating. It was, you know, from day to day, it was becoming back to worse.
So, the Kaviraj who was treating him had advised him complete rest.
He should not travel because even till 1977, he was travelling.
So, I don't remember the month, there was a Rathayatra in London.
And by then, there were hundreds and thousands of devotees.
And each one of them wanting to have darshan of Prabhupada,
have a glimpse of Prabhupada, hear from Prabhupada.
So, they had organized a very, very big Rathayatra festival in London.
And all the devotees there, the leaders there, they were calling Prabhupada.
They were writing letters and calling Prabhupada that,
Please, Prabhupada, come. It will be very inspiring for devotees to have you here.
The whole festival will be surcharged when you are here.
And here, Kaviraj is saying that you should not be moving, you should not be travelling.
So, one side, there was a pressure for them to come.
And the secretaries and the senior devotees who were attending Prabhupada, they were always some kind of a pushback.
No, no, no, Prabhupada cannot come. Prabhupada's health is not okay. Prabhupada is advised not to travel.
And the devotees, please, please, and then one of the devotees called up and said,
Even if he cannot speak, let him just come. Just by seeing him, just by having a glimpse of him, the devotees will be enthused.
Let him just come. Let him not speak. Let him not travel. Just let him come.
So, when Prabhupada came to know that devotees are so eagerly waiting for him to come, he decides, okay, I'll go.
Imagine, Kaviraj is saying, you cannot come.
You cannot travel. You should take bed rest. He says, I'll travel.
So, Prabhupada went to London. This was the last time.
And from the airport, Prabhupada was taken to Bhaktivedanta Manor in the car.
Finally, from the car, because Prabhupada could not walk, so a palanquin was brought, some kind of a palanquin.
Prabhupada was shifted from the car into the palanquin.
And the palanquin was brought into the temple hall.
And as soon as the palanquin was brought, Prabhupada came in.
And as usual, Govindam Adipurusham song was played.
So, Prabhupada was very weak. You know, with some support, he was sitting on the palanquin, hand folded.
And while the Govindam Adipurusham song was going on, profusely tears were flowing from his eyes.
And the devotees who were carrying the palanquin, looking back, continuously tears were gushing through the eyes of Prabhupada, even his kurta became completely wet.
And that song got over, so Prabhupada indicated again. Again that song was played.
And Prabhupada is just completely absorbed, thinking, seeing the deities having darshan.
And tears are flowing from his eyes.
So the condition was that Prabhupada would not speak.
That was agreed upon. Prabhupada would just come, so that devotees can have darshan and, you know, they are enthused.
That was the condition. But nevertheless, some devotee brought the mike.
Maybe Prabhupada wants to speak something.
So Prabhupada got the mike. The mike was brought before his mouth, hands folded.
Tears flowing from his eyes.
And he says, simply love Krishna.
Again, tears are flowing and the mike was taken off.
These are three words Prabhupada spoke, all the way he went to London.
And simply love Krishna.
Now, it is said that when a pure devotee speaks, just like here it is said,
if one meekly and submissively hears about the activities of the Lord from a realized soul,
we can also speak big, big lectures. No transformation will happen.
A pure devotee speaks just few words.
It evokes transcendental emotions.
I spoke the same pastime last Sunday in Mumbai.
And one person, after the session, he said, I am very touched.
This particular, this thing, that is, purity is the force.
So here it says,
when one hears about the activities of the Lord from a realized soul,
he will be able to conquer the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
who is unconquerable by any other process.
Hearing or associating with devotees is the most important function for self-realization.
Hearing and associating with devotees from a realized soul.
Everyone can say, depend on Krishna, remember Krishna.
We should serve Krishna.
But what is going on in our heart?
What is our realization?
To that extent, we will be able, Krishna shakti vina nahi tare pravartanat.
What's going on in our heart?
So that's why for a devotee, it's not just preaching alone.
Janma sarthaka kari karopakaar.
To the extent we are realized, to the extent we have imbibed those instructions,
what's going on in our heart, to that extent we will be able to touch others.
And for that, we don't require bombastic words, we don't require eloquent speaking,
we require realizations.
If one meekly and submissively hears about the activities of the Lord from a realized soul,
he will be able to conquer the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
who is unconquerable by any other process.
Hearing or associating with devotees is the most important function for self-realization.
That's why we should be very, very cautious when we do our morning sadhana, chanting, reading or hearing bhagavatam.
One day it's okay, maybe we are tired, we are sleepy.
One day it's okay, maybe we couldn't chant properly, the mind is agitated.
But if it is happening day after day, day after day, and it doesn't affect me,
I am not bothered about it.
Sometimes devotees get into that, maybe day after day,
or chanting time, feeling sleepy and then talking to someone.
One should do something.
One day it's okay, maybe we are not in control of our body, the body is tired or whatever it is.
But if it is happening day after day, there is something really wrong.
And we have to do something.
Yena, tena, prakara, rena.
Somehow or the other.
Even if we have to do some material gymnastics.
In terms of washing our face, or do something, or breathing, or whatever it is.
Because these are the foundational building blocks.
Hearing from a realized soul, hearing the holy name of the Lord, hearing the Lord who has come in the form of His holy name.
And unless we have this spiritual strength, here it is said, modes of material nature are waiting.
And we have observed, it is on a day to day basis, one day chanting is not okay, the whole day you will see your mind is powerful.
It is like the medicine.
So devotees have to be very conscientious, very alert, very sincere.
Sometimes very serious.
We also, because we have been asked to do it, we do it.
We should understand, many times, now I realize, when I was studying, did my engineering, computer science.
We were just simply studying the books.
There was no overall picture.
You know, you are going depth of networking and computer organization.
One simple question I was asked in an interview.
How does a computer work?
I fumbled giving the answer.
Because sometimes you lose the overall purpose of what we are doing.
We are here in the process of purification.
Anarthanivritti.
To revive our lost Krishna consciousness.
And when we revive that, Krishna says, one achieves my association in this very life.
For I am the Supreme.
And it is not like we have to wait for many, many lifetimes.
If we are sincere, if we are conscientious, if we are very determined,
we are always in the mode of how I can do better, how I can do better, how I can improve.
In this very lifetime we can purify.
In another lecture Prabhupada says,
you know, we know the world today is very different from what it was 20 years back.
And the world 20 years back is very different from what it was 40 years back.
The world is rapidly changing.
The changes which has happened in the last 10 years has not happened in the last century.
And the changes are not very pleasant as far as practice of Krishna consciousness is concerned.
Imagine if we don't finish this process in this life and we again take birth
with the Reliance Jio, unlimited browsing,
streaming live videos.
There is no guarantee that we will,
the Maya's attraction, the allurements will be much, much higher.
So that way we should be very inspired that this very lifetime Krishna is saying here,
my association in this very lifetime for I am the Supreme Personality.
You have got it.
So we will stop here.
Granth Raj Srimad Bhagavatam ki Shila Prabhupada ki.
